key_metrics: Operating income of $17.42 billion (flat YoY compared to $17.41 billion)
Diluted EPS of $1.95 (up from $1.43 in Q3 2024)
9-month YTD Capital Expenditures of $120.13 billion (up 72.2% YoY)
qoq_changes: Total assets increased to $727.92 billion from $624.89 billion at year-end 2024
Cash and cash equivalents decreased to $66.92 billion from $78.78 billion at year-end 2024
Long-term debt decreased slightly to $50.74 billion from $52.62 billion at year-end 2024
new_risks: Significant acceleration in capital expenditures and technology/infrastructure costs (up 30.2% YoY), likely pressuring near-term free cash flow margins
Total operating expenses grew 15% YoY, outpacing revenue growth and resulting in flat operating income despite strong top-line growth
management_tone: Bullish on growth but investment-heavy, evidenced by strong double-digit revenue and net income growth paired with massive infrastructure spending.
analyst_note: Amazon's Q3 2025 shows robust top-line momentum and bottom-line expansion, but flat operating income and a massive $120B YTD CapEx bill highlight an aggressive, margin-dilutive investment cycle likely tied to generative AI and AWS infrastructure.
